fields.description:
## Why
Two low-code investments that protect the design and cut review cost at source. R-3 (maintainer-facing invariant doc) and R-5 (author-facing guide + tooling) are DISTINCT deliverables -- may split into two child tasks; kept together for context.

## What
- R-3: document the stage-by-stage asymmetric-error invariant in docs/plan-review-gate.md (finder errs-toward-surface; verifier errs-toward-author; DET floor fails-open; claim gate fails-closed; novelty/completion floors fail-toward-KEEP) + a floor-tuning/criteria-authoring checklist note.
- R-5 (SEQUENCE AFTER WS1-8): a longer-form Markdown authoring guide derived from the registry, section-keyed by criterion id. The parity gate (validate_packaged_routing in registry.py) today only checks routing JSON vs CANONICAL_LLM vocab, so R-5 must EXTEND it (or add a new check) to fail when a criterion lacks a guide section; do not assume existing coverage. Decide auto-generated vs hand-maintained-with-parity (recommend generated) and record it.
- rebar explain <criterion-id>: ONE shared library lookup (name it, e.g. rebar.llm.plan_review.registry.explain_criterion) wrapped by (a) a CLI dispatch arm in src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py + native-intercept in _cli/_help.py known_subcommands, (b) an MCP tool in src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py via register_llm_tools. Error states for ALL surfaces: unknown-id (exit non-zero / MCP error payload), malformed-registry, missing backing file.
- Coach anchored deep-links: Pass-4 renderer (passes.py) + CLI text renderer (_cli/_llm_commands.py _render_plan_review_text) emit URL#anchor. BASE URL: defaults to the REBAR_DOCS_URL env var if set, else a repo-relative file:// path to the guide; add the default as a config constant in src/rebar/config.py. ANCHOR RULE: `#<criterion-id lower-cased>` matching the guide's per-criterion heading anchors. Downstream consumer to update: code_review/workflow_ops.py consumes render_coach_notes / coaching[] output.

## Provenance
Gap-report labels (G-*/FP-*/R-*/WS-N) are tags from ~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020), NOT codebase symbols. Symbols introduced by a SIBLING story (e.g. committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim from WS1) are target-state, not pre-existing; the dependency is recorded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] R-3 invariant + tuning checklist in docs/plan-review-gate.md.
- [ ] Registry-derived guide, section-keyed by criterion id; parity check FAILS on a missing/renamed section. Proof: `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing` (or the new check) exits non-zero when a criterion's guide section is removed.
- [ ] rebar explain <criterion-id> in CLI + MCP + library off ONE shared lookup. Proof: `rebar explain F1` prints the section; `rebar explain BOGUS` exits non-zero with a clear message AND the MCP tool returns a structured error; unit tests for all three error states (unknown-id, malformed-registry, missing-file) across CLI + MCP.
- [ ] Base URL default (REBAR_DOCS_URL env else relative file://) as a config.py constant; anchor rule `#<criterion-id>`; review-plan --help epilog carries the base URL.
- [ ] Coach output emits stable anchored deep-links; code_review/workflow_ops.py still consumes coaching[] without breakage. Proof: `pytest -k coach_deeplink` green (asserts a URL#anchor is emitted and the downstream consumer parses it).
